text.title: Stage Checklists, ICE Rules, and Antipatterns
text.summary: Four stage-specific optimization checklists, mandatory ICE ranking, mobile segmentation rule, statistical significance enforcement, and antipatterns.

section.title: Stage Checklists
section.p: Landing page checklist: clear headline (1 benefit, under 10 words); subheadline names target audience; hero image/video shows product in use; social proof above fold; single primary CTA; mobile responsive; loads under 3 seconds.

Signup form checklist: 3 or fewer fields for initial signup; value proposition visible near form; social login options (Google, GitHub); progress indicator if multi-step; clear error messages; "what happens next" explanation.

Checkout checklist: guest checkout available; all costs shown upfront (no surprise fees); multiple payment methods; security badges visible; easy to edit cart; clear refund policy; auto-save progress.

Onboarding checklist: welcome with clear single next step; 3 or fewer onboarding screens; skip option always available; core value demonstrated immediately; contextual tips triggered by actions (not upfront tutorial); progress saved automatically.

rule.statement: Every tactic in the test backlog must be pinned to: (1) a measurable stage event, (2) a current baseline rate, and (3) a target lift band. Reject any tactic without all three.
rule.rationale: "Improve copy" without a conversion metric and baseline is not a testable hypothesis. Unpinned tactics produce inconclusive tests and wasted sprint capacity.

rule.statement: Always test and report tactics on mobile and desktop segments separately. Never report a combined conversion rate when segments represent materially different surfaces.
rule.rationale: A form-field reduction that lifts desktop by 20% can drop mobile by 10% if the mobile layout does not adapt. Combined metrics mask the mobile regression.

rule.statement: Cap concurrent A/B tests on the same page to one. Do not start the next test until the current one has reached statistical significance (95% confidence, minimum sample size met).
rule.rationale: Running two tests on the same page at the same time prevents isolating the effect of either change. This is the "testing too many things" error that inflates false-positive rate.

rule.statement: Urgency and scarcity tactics (countdown timers, low-stock messages) must reflect real constraints. Never generate fake countdown timers or inventory counts.
rule.rationale: Fake urgency is deceptive, creates legal and brand risk, and undermines trust when users discover the timer resets on refresh.

antipattern.description: Optimizing small funnel leaks before fixing the largest drop-off stage.
antipattern.reason: Fixing a 5% checkout drop-off while a 50% bounce rate on the landing page exists wastes all effort — the checkout improvement cannot compound when most users never reach checkout.

antipattern.description: Recommending "remove all form fields" without a recovery plan for the trimmed fields.
antipattern.reason: Progressive profiling — gathering removed fields after signup via in-app prompts — must be specified alongside any field-reduction recommendation or the data is permanently lost.

antipattern.description: Auto-publishing landing page variants without experiment flag and human review.
antipattern.reason: Direct production changes bypass the A/B test framework, contaminate existing tests, and prevent clean rollback if the change causes a regression.

text.reference: Playwright signup form auditor: field count, social login, CTA, load time
